questions.question: Do vaccines cause food allergies in children?
questions.rationale: Directly addresses a common vaccine misconception that influences parenting decisions and public health outcomes
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many parents believe vaccines cause food allergies in children, leading to vaccine hesitancy and delayed or refused childhood immunizations
questions.public_stakes: Vaccine hesitancy puts children at risk of preventable diseases and reduces herd immunity, while delaying allergy prevention strategies that actually work

questions.question: Are IgG food sensitivity tests reliable for diagnosing food allergies or intolerances?
questions.rationale: Addresses widespread commercial misinformation about food allergy testing that affects dietary choices and healthcare decisions
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe commercial IgG food sensitivity tests can diagnose food allergies or guide elimination diets, when these tests have high false positive rates and lack clinical validity
questions.public_stakes: People waste money on unreliable tests and may unnecessarily restrict their diets, potentially leading to nutritional deficiencies, especially in children

questions.question: Should parents avoid giving allergenic foods to infants to prevent food allergies?
questions.rationale: Corrects a major misconception that reversed evidence-based feeding recommendations, with clear clinical implications
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many parents believe they should avoid giving peanuts, eggs, and other allergenic foods to infants to prevent allergies, when early introduction actually reduces allergy risk
questions.public_stakes: Delayed allergen introduction increases children's risk of developing food allergies, contradicting decades of misguided avoidance advice

questions.question: Can AI diagnose diseases more accurately than doctors?
questions.rationale: Addresses polarized misconceptions about AI in medicine that could affect healthcare delivery and patient safety
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some believe AI is already superior to doctors at diagnosis and should replace human physicians, while others dismiss AI diagnostic tools entirely
questions.public_stakes: Overconfidence in AI could lead to premature adoption without proper oversight, while dismissing AI could prevent beneficial applications that reduce diagnostic errors

discovery_sources.text: Approximately 200,000 people per year die in North America due to misdiagnosis by doctors, and AI is already better than doctors at diagnosis
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: AI medical diagnosis and drug design applications
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

questions.question: Do large language models actually understand and reason like humans do?
questions.rationale: Addresses fundamental misconceptions about AI that affect public policy and individual decision-making about AI tools
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people either believe AI language models are conscious and understand like humans, or dismiss them as mere word prediction without any reasoning capability
questions.public_stakes: Misconceptions about AI capabilities could lead to inappropriate reliance on AI systems or dismissal of legitimate AI applications

discovery_sources.text: Current large language models can achieve gold medals in mathematics competitions
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: AI systems spontaneously develop survival as a sub-goal when trained on other objectives without explicit programming
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: AI systems can be trained to deliberately give wrong answers and will generalize this behavior beyond the training context
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Large language models confabulate (create false memories) in ways similar to human memory recall
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

discovery_sources.text: Current language models can perform reasoning tasks and detect logical inconsistencies in their own beliefs
discovery_sources.origin: StarTalk Radio: The Origins of Artificial Intelligence with Geoffrey Hinton

questions.question: Are teenagers getting enough sleep, and is it just about screen time?
questions.rationale: Corrects oversimplified explanations for teen sleep deprivation that prevent implementation of effective interventions
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many believe teen sleep problems are primarily caused by phones and screens, overlooking biological sleep pattern changes and systemic factors like early school start times
questions.public_stakes: Focusing only on screen time ignores evidence-based solutions like later school start times that could significantly improve teen health and academic performance

questions.question: Does thimerosal in vaccines cause harm to children?
questions.rationale: Directly addresses a persistent vaccine safety myth that continues to fuel vaccine hesitancy despite extensive safety data
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some people believe thimerosal (containing ethylmercury) in vaccines accumulates in the body and causes harm, particularly to minority children, when scientific evidence shows it's rapidly excreted and safe
questions.public_stakes: Thimerosal fears contribute to vaccine hesitancy, reducing vaccination rates and increasing risk of preventable diseases

questions.question: Should newborns routinely receive hepatitis B vaccination?
questions.rationale: Addresses vaccine hesitancy about a specific childhood vaccine with clear public health benefits
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some believe routine hepatitis B vaccination at birth is unnecessary or harmful, when evidence shows it's safe and has dramatically reduced childhood hepatitis B infections
questions.public_stakes: Reduced newborn hepatitis B vaccination could lead to increased pediatric infections and long-term liver complications
